title: Coconut layer cake
subtitle: A tall white sponge with coconut filling and toasted coconut sides
excerpt: Three layers of a soft white butter sponge with coconut milk in the batter, filled and frosted with a coconut cream cheese frosting, and pressed all over with toasted desiccated coconut.
sourceNotes: Coconut layer cake is a staple of American Southern baking, particularly associated with Easter and summer celebrations. The combination of coconut milk in the sponge and toasted coconut on the exterior appears in Southern food writing from the mid-twentieth century. The recipe here follows the classic American approach adapted for metric weights.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze the unfrosted cake layers. The coconut frosting does not freeze well.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The assembled cake keeps refrigerated for 2 days. The toasted coconut on the outside should be added on the day of serving.

glossaryTerms.definition: The liquid pressed from the white flesh of mature coconuts, blended with water. In baking, full-fat coconut milk adds moisture and a delicate coconut flavour. It is thicker and fattier than coconut water. Shake the tin before opening as the fat and liquid separate when cold.

content.text: in the sponge batter replaces most of the dairy milk and gives the crumb a soft, slightly dense texture and a clean coconut flavour that carries through the whole cake. Toast the desiccated coconut for the exterior before the cake bakes; it cools while you bake, and pressing it on while the frosting is fresh makes it adhere more reliably.
Toast the coconut and bake the layers

Spread 150 g desiccated coconut on a baking tray and toast at 180°C for 5 to 7 minutes until pale gold, shaking the tray once. Watch it carefully; coconut turns from golden to brown very quickly. Allow to cool. Grease and line three 23 cm round tins. Beat the butter and sugar until pale and fluffy. Add the eggs one at a time, then fold in the flour, baking powder, and salt in alternation with the coconut milk. Fold in the 80 g untoasted desiccated coconut and vanilla. Divide between the tins and bake for 25 to 30 minutes until a skewer comes out clean. Cool on racks.

Beat the cream cheese and butter until smooth. Add the sifted icing sugar in three additions and beat until thick and spreadable. Add the coconut extract. Refrigerate the frosting for 20 minutes if it feels too soft to spread. Stack the three layers with frosting between each, then coat the top and sides generously. While the frosting is still soft, press the toasted coconut all over the top and sides with clean hands, patting it in so it adheres.
